successCriteria.description: `src/shared/types/leave.types.ts` exists and exports all 7 TypeScript enums: `LeaveType` (ANNUAL, SICK, MATERNITY, PATERNITY, UNPAID, OTHER), `LeaveStatus` (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED), `NotificationType` (LEAVE_REQUESTED, LEAVE_APPROVED, LEAVE_REJECTED, LEAVE_CANCELLED), `AuditAction` (CREATED, UPDATED, DELETED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED), `EntityType` (LEAVE_REQUEST, LEAVE_BALANCE, LEAVE_POLICY, EMPLOYEE), `EmployeeRole` (EMPLOYEE, MANAGER, HR_ADMIN), `EmploymentStatus` (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, TERMINATED, ON_LEAVE)

`src/shared/error.types.ts` exists and exports 4 error classes: `NotFoundError` (extends Error, constructor takes `entityName: string` and `id: string`), `ValidationError` (extends Error, constructor takes `message: string` and optional `fieldErrors` array), `ForbiddenError` (extends Error, constructor takes `message: string`), `ConflictError` (extends Error, constructor takes `message: string`)

`src/shared/base.repository.ts` exists and exports an abstract class `BaseRepository<T>` that accepts a Knex instance via constructor and declares abstract/common CRUD method signatures: `findById`, `findAll`, `create`, `update`, `delete`

`tests/unit/shared/error.types.test.ts` exists with Vitest unit tests covering all 4 error classes: verifies correct instance construction, proper Error inheritance (`instanceof Error`), correct `name` property, and correct property assignment (`entityName`/`id` for NotFoundError, `message`/`fieldErrors` for ValidationError, `message` for ForbiddenError and ConflictError)

constraints: All enums must use TypeScript `enum` keyword (not const objects or union types), as they are consumed by both runtime code and Zod `nativeEnum` validators in later phases
Error classes must use `Object.setPrototypeOf(this, new.target.prototype)` in their constructors to ensure correct prototype chain when targeting ES5/ES6 (TypeScript `extends Error` pattern)
`BaseRepository<T>` must be abstract and must not contain any implementation — only method signatures. The Knex instance is accepted via constructor injection and stored as a protected field
Tests must use Vitest (not Jest), per AGENTS.md and ARCHITECTURE.md reconciliation note #1
All files must use strict TypeScript (no implicit any, strict null checks) per AGENTS.md
No database access, no HTTP handlers, no business logic — this phase is purely shared types, base classes, and error definitions
outOfScope: Phase 2 — Employee model and repository (depends on this phase's enums and base repository)
Phase 3 — LeavePolicy model, repository, service, and index
Phase 6 — AuditLog and Notification models and repositories
Phase 10 — Leave routes with RBAC middleware
Any database migrations, table creation, or seed data
Any business logic, validation rules, or RBAC enforcement

ambiguities.description: Knex type source: The intent says "Use the Knex type from the existing project setup" but `src/shared/db/connection.ts` currently uses `pg` Pool directly with no Knex dependency installed. The ARCHITECTURE.md states Knex is used for migrations and repository layer. The code-agent must either install `knex` as a dependency and import `Knex` from it, or use a different approach.
options: Install knex and @types/knex as dependencies, import `Knex` from 'knex'
Use a generic database client type instead of Knex-specific type
Use `any` typed as Knex (violates no-any constraint)

ambiguities.description: BaseRepository CRUD method signatures: The intent lists method names (`findById`, `findAll`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) but does not specify their parameter types, return types, or whether they return promises. For example: does `findById` accept `string | number` and return `Promise<T | null>`? Does `create` accept `Omit<T, 'id'>` or a dedicated DTO? Does `delete` return `Promise<void>` or `Promise<boolean>`?
options: findById(id: string): Promise<T | null>; findAll(filter?: Partial<T>): Promise<T[]>; create(entity: Omit<T, 'id'>): Promise<T>; update(id: string, entity: Partial<T>): Promise<T>; delete(id: string): Promise<void>
Use generic constraints with Knex.QueryBuilder return types
Leave signatures minimal with `unknown` typed params and let subclasses narrow them

ambiguities.description: ValidationError.fieldErrors element type: The intent says "optional fieldErrors array" but does not specify the shape of each element. Common patterns include `{ field: string; message: string }[]`, `string[]`, or `{ path: string; code: string }[]`.
options: { field: string; message: string }[] — structured field-level errors
string[] — simple list of error messages
{ path: (string | number)[]; message: string }[] — Zod-compatible shape

ambiguities.description: NotFoundError `id` parameter type: The intent says NotFoundError "takes entityName and id" but does not specify whether `id` is `string`, `number`, or `string | number`. This affects type safety in catch blocks across all later phases.
options: id: string — all entity IDs are UUID strings
id: string | number — flexible for different ID types
id: unknown — maximally flexible, caller casts
